source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Johann stacks $1 \times 1$ cubes on the squares of a $4 \times 4$ grid. The diagram on the right shows how many cubes were piled on top of each other on each square of the grid. What will Johann see if he looks from behind (hinten) at the tower?
<image1>
<image2>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'The diagram shows' and asks what Johann will see from behind, which requires visual interpretation of the stacked-cube arrangement
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is not solvable from text alone

multi_step: solving requires reading cube heights on a 4x4 grid from the diagram and then transforming that 3D arrangement into a rear-view projection
the task involves spatial reasoning about visibility/occlusion from a specified viewpoint ('from behind') rather than a single direct lookup
multiple-choice format with visual options suggests comparison between a derived rear view and candidate images
verifiability: answer_manifest shows answer_present=true and answer_type='choice', so there is a definite target answer
the provided answer_text='(C)' is concise and easy to compare against a predicted option label
